full_script: Rome didn’t just lose battles— it lost the system that made it unbeatable, not overnight, but after ruling the Mediterranean for centuries— even as the empire shattered, everyone still wanted to inherit Rome’s power. Why? Because the Roman 'system' was more powerful than just armies. Modern economists call this institutional decline— so did Rome collapse from enemies—or from within? Drop your take below.
